item.title: Version 0.3.0
item.description:
v0.3.0 — native voice-memo recording from the menubar

  • Menubar recorder. Click the waveform icon → Start voice memo to record on the spot. Pause/resume as many times as you like, then Save and send to capture. Menubar icon goes red while recording and amber while paused so a live recording is impossible to miss.
  • Context-capture sheet opens on save:
    • Language picker — Hebrew is the default (Hebrew-pinned whisper handles English mixed into Hebrew speech cleanly; auto-detect collapses to English).
    • Calendar event auto-suggested from your Google Calendar for meetings overlapping the recording window.
    • Attendees pre-fill from the selected event; editable add/remove.
    • Notes textarea → context.md the extract pass reads.
    • Attachments — drop PDFs/decks/prior notes into the bundle.
  • Multilingual whisper. Auto-detects language and picks the multilingual large-v3 model for anything non-English instead of the English-optimized large-v3-turbo. Hebrew, Spanish, etc. actually work now.
  • Calendar binding. Picking an event populates context.md frontmatter with event_id, attendees, link — so extract routes commitments to the right people and the routed meeting note links back to Calendar.

Under the hood

  • Subprocess PATH augmentation so doctor + server find Homebrew tools when launched via LSOpen.
  • Server orphan-killer fixes "Address already in use" after force-quit.
  • Design-system assets (Ploni fonts + tokens.css) now bundled instead of gitignored.
  • INTAKE_DIR → CAPTURES_DIR rename leak fixed (was causing /api/library to 500).

item.title: Version 0.2.2
item.description:
v0.2.2 — first fully-working release

  • Menubar buttons now respond to clicks. v0.2.0 and v0.2.1 had a broken responder chain caused by Sparkle's auto-check window opening at launch; this release fixes it by deferring Sparkle's startup until user-triggered check.
  • Menubar style switched from .menu to .window popover for reliable action dispatch on macOS Tahoe 26.
  • Default server port: 5055 (was 8765, which collided with the brain store http.server).
  • --port and --no-open properly passed to the Python subprocess.
  • Server-error surfacing in the menubar (subprocess stderr is now visible on failure).
  • Build pipeline hardening: build script now fails loudly on Swift compile errors instead of silently shipping the previous cached binary (this was masking ~1 hour of debugging on 2026-06-01).
  • Sparkle 2.x API: updater.start() (was incorrectly startUpdater()).

If you're on v0.2.0 or v0.2.1 and your menubar doesn't respond, redownload from the landing page.
